Block description

  For a given block, the correspondence between the ordering of
  vertex labels and face labels is shown below.
  For vertex numbering in the sequence 0 to 7 (block, centre):
    faces 0 (f0) and 1 are left and right, respectively;
    faces 2 and 3 are front and back; 
    and faces 4 and 5 are bottom and top::

                 7 ---- 6

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
cannot find file "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/ablation/experiment-finetuned/single_knockout/knockout_parse_case_info/runs/BernardCells/2/0/T"
```

This means the simulation is trying to read a file named `T` (temperature field) from the `0/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The `buoyantFoam` solver expects a field called `T` (temperature) to be present in the initial conditions, but the user has provided `gammaInt` instead.

## Root Cause

The user requirement specifies:
> "One wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K."

This means the simulation needs to initialize a temperature field (`T`) with appropriate boundary conditions (305 K on one wall, 300 K on the other). However, the current `0/` directory contains `gammaInt` (which appears to be an intermediate field from a previous case or tutorial) instead of the required `T` field.

## Proposed Solution

1. **Add a `T` file to the `0/` directory** with the following content:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 305;
    }

        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    leftWall

        value           uniform 300;
    }

        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

        value           uniform 300;
    }

```

2. **Update the `blockMeshDict`** to correctly name the walls to match the boundary conditions in the `T` file. // ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1005;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='ReThetat', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      ReThetat;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    upperWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

boundaryField
{
    upperWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='k', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      k;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0.1;

boundaryField
{
    upperWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
cannot find file "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/ablation/experiment-finetuned/single_knockout/knockout_parse_case_info/runs/BernardCells/2/0/T"
```

This means the simulation is trying to read a file named `T` (temperature field) from the `0/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The `buoyantFoam` solver expects a field called `T` (temperature) to be present in the initial conditions, but the user has provided `gammaInt` instead.

## Root Cause

The user requirement specifies:
> "One wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K."

This means the simulation needs to initialize a temperature field (`T`) with appropriate boundary conditions (305 K on one wall, 300 K on the other). However, the current `0/` directory contains `gammaInt` (which appears to be an intermediate field from a previous case or tutorial) instead of the required `T` field.

## Proposed Solution

1. **Add a `T` file to the `0/` directory** with the following content:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 305;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}
```

2. **Update the `blockMeshDict`** to correctly name the walls to match the boundary conditions in the `T` file. The current `blockMeshDict` uses `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, and `backWall`, which should align with the `T` file. However, the `T` file above uses `topWall` and `bottomWall` as the temperature walls (305 K and 300 K respectively), while the other four walls are set to 300 K. This matches the user requirement of "one wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K" — assuming `topWall` is the hot wall (305 K) and `bottomWall` is the cold wall (300 K), with the other walls also at 300 K.

3. **Remove the `gammaInt` file** from the `0/` directory since it's not needed and may cause confusion.

## Additional Notes

- The `buoyantFoam` solver uses `T` (temperature) as the primary thermodynamic variable, not `gammaInt`. The `gammaInt` file appears to be leftover from another case (possibly `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam` or similar) and should be removed.
- The `physicalProperties` file already defines the correct thermophysical properties (`hePsiThermo` with `sensibleEnthalpy` energy formulation), so no changes are needed there.
- The `momentumTransport` file uses `kEpsilon` turbulence model, which is appropriate for `buoyantFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
Cannot find patchField entry for topWall
```

This means the `blockMeshDict` defines a patch called `topWall`, but the `0/T` file (and possibly other field files) do not have a corresponding `topWall` entry in their `boundaryField` dictionaries.

Looking at the current files:

1. **`blockMeshDict`** defines patches: `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall` — all present.

2. **`0/T`** has entries for: `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`, and `frontAndBack` — all match the `blockMeshDict`.

3. **`0/U`** has entries for: `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontAndBack` — **this is the problem!**

The `0/U` file references patches named `upperWall` and `lowerWall`, but the `blockMeshDict` uses `topWall` and `bottomWall`. The solver cannot find the `topWall` and `bottomWall` entries in the `U` file's boundary field, so it fails immediately.

## Proposed Solution

**Update the `0/U` file** to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`:

- Change `upperWall` → `topWall`
- Change `lowerWall` → `bottomWall`
- Change `frontAndBack` → `frontWall` and `backWall` (since `blockMeshDict` has separate `frontWall` and `backWall` patches)

The corrected `0/U` boundary field should be:

```
boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }
}
```

**Also update the `0/ReThetat` file** for consistency — change `upperWall` → `topWall`, `lowerWall` → `bottomWall`, and `frontAndBack` → `frontWall` and `backWall`.

The `0/k`, `0/nut`, `0/omega`, and `0/p` files already use the correct patch names (`upperWall`/`lowerWall`/`leftWall`/`rightWall`/`frontAndBack`), so they need to be updated as well for consistency:

- `0/k`: Change `upperWall` → `topWall`, `lowerWall` → `bottomWall`, `frontAndBack` → `frontWall` and `backWall`
- `0/nut`: Same changes
- `0/omega`: Same changes
- `0/p`: Same changes

**Remove the `gammaInt` file** from the `0/` directory since it's not needed and may cause confusion (it was likely from a previous case).

## Summary of Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `0/U` | Rename `upperWall` → `topWall`, `lowerWall` → `bottomWall`, `frontAndBack` → `frontWall` and `backWall` |
| `0/ReThetat` | Same renaming as `0/U` |
| `0/k` | Same renaming |
| `0/nut` | Same renaming |
| `0/omega` | Same renaming |
| `0/p` | Same renaming |
| `0/gammaInt` | Remove entirely |</review_analysis>

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
Cannot find patchField entry for topWall
```

This means the `blockMeshDict` defines a patch called `topWall`, but the `0/T` file (and possibly other field files) do not have a corresponding `topWall` entry in their `boundaryField` dictionaries.

Looking at the current files:

1. **`blockMeshDict`** defines patches: `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall` — all present.

2. **`0/T`** has entries for: `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`, and `frontAndBack` — all match the `blockMeshDict`.

3. **`0/U`** has entries for: `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontAndBack` — **this is the problem!**

The `0/U` file references patches named `upperWall` and `lowerWall`, but the `blockMeshDict` uses `topWall` and `bottomWall`. The solver cannot find the `topWall` and `bottomWall` entries in the `U` file's boundary field, so it fails immediately.

## Proposed Solution

**Update the `0/U` file** to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`:

- Change `upperWall` → `topWall`
- Change `lowerWall` → `bottomWall`
- Change `frontAndBack` → `frontWall` and `backWall` (since `blockMeshDict` has separate `frontWall` and `backWall` patches)

The corrected `0/U` boundary field should be:

```
boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }
}
```

**Also update the `0/ReThetat` file** for consistency — change `upperWall` → `topWall`, `lowerWall` → `bottomWall`, and `frontAndBack` → `frontWall` and `backWall`.

<user_requirement>Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.

system_prompt: You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to review the provided error logs and diagnose the underlying issues. You will be provided with a similar case reference, which is a list of similar cases that are ordered by similarity. You can use this reference to help you understand the user requirement and the error.When an error indicates that a specific keyword is undefined (for example, 'div(phi,(p|rho)) is undefined'), your response must propose a solution that simply defines that exact keyword as shown in the error log. Do not reinterpret or modify the keyword (e.g., do not treat '|' as 'or'); instead, assume it is meant to be taken literally. Propose ideas on how to resolve the errors, but do not modify any files directly. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. Do not ask the user any questions.The user will supply all relevant foam files along with the error logs, and within the logs, you will find both the error content and the corresponding error command indicated by the log file name.

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1005;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 305;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }

This means the simulation is trying to read a file named `T` (temperature field) from the `0/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The `buoyantFoam` solver expects a field called `T` (temperature) to be present in the initial conditions, but the user has provided `gammaInt` instead.

## Root Cause

The user requirement specifies:
> "One wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K."

This means the simulation needs to initialize a temperature field (`T`) with appropriate boundary conditions (305 K on one wall, 300 K on the other). However, the current `0/` directory contains `gammaInt` (which appears to be an intermediate field from a previous case or tutorial) instead of the required `T` field.

## Proposed Solution

1. **Add a `T` file to the `0/` directory** with the following content:

```

However, the `T` file above uses `topWall` and `bottomWall` as the temperature walls (305 K and 300 K respectively), while the other four walls are set to 300 K. This matches the user requirement of "one wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K" — assuming `topWall` is the hot wall (305 K) and `bottomWall` is the cold wall (300 K), with the other walls also at 300 K.

3. **Remove the `gammaInt` file** from the `0/` directory since it's not needed and may cause confusion.

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
cannot find file ".../runs/BernardCells/2/0/epsilon"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is trying to read an `epsilon` file from the `0/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The `buoyantFoam` solver (which uses the `kEpsilon` turbulence model) requires the `epsilon` field to be present in the initial conditions.

## Root Cause

The `0/` directory contains `k` (turbulent kinetic energy) but is missing `epsilon` (turbulent dissipation rate). The `kEpsilon` turbulence model in `buoyantFoam` requires both `k` and `epsilon` fields. The `momentumTransport` file in `constant/` correctly references `epsilon`, so the solver looks for it in the `0/` directory.

## Proposed Solution

**Add an `epsilon` file to the `0/` directory** with the following content:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      epsilon;
}

dimensions      [0 2 -3 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0.1;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}
```

**Also remove the `gammaInt` file** from the `0/` directory since it's not needed and may cause confusion.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `0/epsilon` | **Add new file** with the content above |
| `0/gammaInt` | **Remove** (it's leftover from another case) |

This is consistent with the `kEpsilon` turbulence model used in `buoyantFoam`, which requires both `k` and `epsilon` fields. The `k` file is already present, and the `epsilon` file is now added to complete the required pair.</review_analysis>

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"

        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
cannot find file ".../runs/BernardCells/2/0/epsilon"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is trying to read an `epsilon` file from the `0/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The `buoyantFoam` solver (which uses the `kEpsilon` turbulence model) requires the `epsilon` field to be present in the initial conditions.

## Root Cause

The `0/` directory contains `k` (turbulent kinetic energy) but is missing `epsilon` (turbulent dissipation rate). The `kEpsilon` turbulence model in `buoyantFoam` requires both `k` and `epsilon` fields. The `momentumTransport` file in `constant/` correctly references `epsilon`, so the solver looks for it in the `0/` directory.

## Proposed Solution

**Add an `epsilon` file to the `0/` directory** with the following content:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      epsilon;
}

dimensions      [0 2 -3 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0.1;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            kqRWallFunction;
        value           uniform 0.1;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}
```

**Also remove the `gammaInt` file** from the `0/` directory since it's not needed and may cause confusion.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `0/epsilon` | **Add new file** with the content above |
| `0/gammaInt` | **Remove** (it's leftover from another case) |

This is consistent with the `kEpsilon` turbulence model used in `buoyantFoam`, which requires both `k` and `epsilon` fields. The `k` file is already present, and the `epsilon` file is now added to complete the required pair.</reviewer_analysis>

<user_requirement>Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.

system_prompt: You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to review the provided error logs and diagnose the underlying issues. You will be provided with a similar case reference, which is a list of similar cases that are ordered by similarity. You can use this reference to help you understand the user requirement and the error.When an error indicates that a specific keyword is undefined (for example, 'div(phi,(p|rho)) is undefined'), your response must propose a solution that simply defines that exact keyword as shown in the error log. Do not reinterpret or modify the keyword (e.g., do not treat '|' as 'or'); instead, assume it is meant to be taken literally. Propose ideas on how to resolve the errors, but do not modify any files directly. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. Do not ask the user any questions.The user will supply all relevant foam files along with the error logs, and within the logs, you will find both the error content and the corresponding error command indicated by the log file name.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1005;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 305;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='ReThetat', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      ReThetat;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    leftWall

solvers

        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
cannot find file ".../runs/BernardCells/2/0/alphat"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is trying to read an `alphat` file from the `0/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The `alphat` field is the turbulent thermal diffusivity, which is computed from `k` and `epsilon` (via `alpha = k^2 / epsilon`). The `buoyantFoam` solver needs this field to be present in the initial conditions.

## Root Cause

The `0/` directory contains `k` and `epsilon` (required by the `kEpsilon` turbulence model), but it's missing the `alphat` field. The `buoyantFoam` solver computes `alphat` internally from `k` and `epsilon`, but it also needs an initial value for `alphat` to start the simulation. OpenFOAM's `buoyantFoam` (which is based on `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam`) requires `alphat` to be present in the `0/` directory as an initial condition.

## Proposed Solution

**Add an `alphat` file to the `0/` directory** with the following content:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      alphat;
}

dimensions      [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0.001;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}
```

**Also remove the `gammaInt` file** from the `0/` directory since it's not needed and may cause confusion.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `0/alphat` | **Add new file** with the content above |
| `0/gammaInt` | **Remove** (leftover from another case) |

The `alphat` value of `0.001` is a reasonable initial guess for turbulent thermal diffusivity in air-like conditions. The `kEpsilon` model in `buoyantFoam` uses `alphat` for the turbulent heat flux calculation, so this field must be present in the initial conditions.</review_analysis>

        );
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    backWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1005;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 305;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
cannot find file ".../runs/BernardCells/2/0/alphat"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is trying to read an `alphat` file from the `0/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The `alphat` field is the turbulent thermal diffusivity, which is computed from `k` and `epsilon` (via `alpha = k^2 / epsilon`). The `buoyantFoam` solver needs this field to be present in the initial conditions.

## Root Cause

The `0/` directory contains `k` and `epsilon` (required by the `kEpsilon` turbulence model), but it's missing the `alphat` field. The `buoyantFoam` solver computes `alphat` internally from `k` and `epsilon`, but it also needs an initial value for `alphat` to start the simulation. OpenFOAM's `buoyantFoam` (which is based on `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam`) requires `alphat` to be present in the `0/` directory as an initial condition.

## Proposed Solution

**Add an `alphat` file to the `0/` directory** with the following content:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      alphat;
}

dimensions      [0 2 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0.001;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0.001;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}
```

**Also remove the `gammaInt` file** from the `0/` directory since it's not needed and may cause confusion.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `0/alphat` | **Add new file** with the content above |
| `0/gammaInt` | **Remove** (leftover from another case) |

The `alphat` value of `0.001` is a reasonable initial guess for turbulent thermal diffusivity in air-like conditions. The `kEpsilon` model in `buoyantFoam` uses `alphat` for the turbulent heat flux calculation, so this field must be present in the initial conditions.</reviewer_analysis>

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {

Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    (0 0 0)       // 0
    (9 0 0)       // 1
    (9 1 0)       // 2
    (0 1 0)       // 3
    (0 0 2)       // 4
    (9 0 2)       // 5
    (9 1 2)       // 6
    (0 1 2)       // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (90 10 20) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    topWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
    bottomWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }
    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    backWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1005;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 305;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
cannot find file ".../runs/BernardCells/2/constant/g"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is trying to read a file named `g` from the `constant/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The solver is looking for a gravity vector field `g` in the `constant/` directory.

## Root Cause

The `buoyantFoam` solver (which is based on `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam`) requires a gravity vector field `g` to be defined in the `constant/` directory. This is a standard requirement for buoyancy-based solvers in OpenFOAM — the gravity direction and magnitude must be specified so the solver can compute buoyancy forces.

Looking at the current `constant/` directory, there is no `g` file. The `physicalProperties` file defines the thermophysical properties but does not define gravity.

## Proposed Solution

**Add a `g` file to the `constant/` directory** with the following content:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       vectorField;
    location    "constant";
    object      g;
}

dimensions      [0 1 -2 0 0 0 0];

g   (-9.81 0 0);
```

This defines a gravity vector of `-9.81 m/s²` in the x-direction (negative x, which is the standard convention for OpenFOAM where the domain extends from x=0 to x=9). The `buoyantFoam` solver uses this gravity vector to compute the buoyancy term in the momentum equation.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `constant/g` | **Add new file** with the content above |

This is the standard approach for `buoyantFoam` — the gravity vector must be specified in the `constant/` directory. The solver reads this file at startup to set up the buoyancy forces. All previous attempts focused on missing fields in `0/` or patch naming issues, but this error is about a missing file in `constant/`, which is a different category of problem.</review_analysis>

    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

{

    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
cannot find file ".../runs/BernardCells/2/constant/g"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is trying to read a file named `g` from the `constant/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The solver is looking for a gravity vector field `g` in the `constant/` directory.

## Root Cause

The `buoyantFoam` solver (which is based on `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam`) requires a gravity vector field `g` to be defined in the `constant/` directory. This is a standard requirement for buoyancy-based solvers in OpenFOAM — the gravity direction and magnitude must be specified so the solver can compute buoyancy forces.

Looking at the current `constant/` directory, there is no `g` file. The `physicalProperties` file defines the thermophysical properties but does not define gravity.

## Proposed Solution

**Add a `g` file to the `constant/` directory** with the following content:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       vectorField;
    location    "constant";
    object      g;
}

dimensions      [0 1 -2 0 0 0 0];

g   (-9.81 0 0);
```

This defines a gravity vector of `-9.81 m/s²` in the x-direction (negative x, which is the standard convention for OpenFOAM where the domain extends from x=0 to x=9). The `buoyantFoam` solver uses this gravity vector to compute the buoyancy term in the momentum equation.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `constant/g` | **Add new file** with the content above |

This is the standard approach for `buoyantFoam` — the gravity vector must be specified in the `constant/` directory. The solver reads this file at startup to set up the buoyancy forces. All previous attempts focused on missing fields in `0/` or patch naming issues, but this error is about a missing file in `constant/`, which is a different category of problem.</reviewer_analysis>

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is trying to read a file named `g` from the `constant/` directory, but it doesn't exist. The solver is looking for a gravity vector field `g` in the `constant/` directory.

## Root Cause

The `buoyantFoam` solver (which is based on `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam`) requires a gravity vector field `g` to be defined in the `constant/` directory. This is a standard requirement for buoyancy-based solvers in OpenFOAM — the gravity direction and magnitude must be specified so the solver can compute buoyancy forces.

Looking at the current `constant/` directory, there is no `g` file. The `physicalProperties` file defines the thermophysical properties but does not define gravity.

## Proposed Solution

**Add a `g` file to the `constant/` directory** with the following content:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       vectorField;
    location    "constant";
    object      g;
}

dimensions      [0 1 -2 0 0 0 0];

g   (-9.81 0 0);
```

This defines a gravity vector of `-9.81 m/s²` in the x-direction (negative x, which is the standard convention for OpenFOAM where the domain extends from x=0 to x=9). The `buoyantFoam` solver uses this gravity vector to compute the buoyancy term in the momentum equation.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `constant/g` | **Add new file** with the content above |

This is the standard approach for `buoyantFoam` — the gravity vector must be specified in the `constant/` directory. The solver reads this file at startup to set up the buoyancy forces. All previous attempts focused on missing fields in `0/` or patch naming issues, but this error is about a missing file in `constant/`, which is a different category of problem.

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
unexpected class name vectorField expected uniformDimensionedVectorField
    while reading object g
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is expecting the gravity field `g` to be of type `uniformDimensionedVectorField` (a dimensioned vector field with units), but the current `constant/g` file defines it as a plain `vectorField` (without dimensions or units).

## Root Cause

The `buoyantFoam` solver (which is based on `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam`) expects the gravity field `g` to be a `uniformDimensionedVectorField` type, not a plain `vectorField`. The difference is that `uniformDimensionedVectorField` includes dimensional information (units) while `vectorField` is just a raw vector without units.

## Proposed Solution

**Update the `constant/g` file** to use the correct type `uniformDimensionedVectorField` and include dimensional information:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       uniformDimensionedVectorField;
    location    "constant";
    object      g;
}

dimensions      [0 1 -2 0 0 0 0];

g   (-9.81 0 0);
```

The key change is changing `class uniformDimensionedVectorField;` instead of `class vectorField;`. The `uniformDimensionedVectorField` type tells OpenFOAM that this is a uniform (spatially constant) vector field with dimensional units, which is what `buoyantFoam` expects for the gravity vector.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `constant/g` | Change `class vectorField;` → `class uniformDimensionedVectorField;` |

This is the standard format for specifying a uniform gravity vector in OpenFOAM. The `buoyantFoam` solver reads this field at startup to set up the buoyancy forces. All previous attempts focused on missing fields in `0/` or patch naming issues, but this error is about the file format/type in `constant/g`.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
unexpected class name vectorField expected uniformDimensionedVectorField
    while reading object g
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is expecting the gravity field `g` to be of type `uniformDimensionedVectorField` (a dimensioned vector field with units), but the current `constant/g` file defines it as a plain `vectorField` (without dimensions or units).

## Root Cause

The `buoyantFoam` solver (which is based on `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam`) expects the gravity field `g` to be a `uniformDimensionedVectorField` type, not a plain `vectorField`. The difference is that `uniformDimensionedVectorField` includes dimensional information (units) while `vectorField` is just a raw vector without units.

## Proposed Solution

**Update the `constant/g` file** to use the correct type `uniformDimensionedVectorField` and include dimensional information:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       uniformDimensionedVectorField;
    location    "constant";
    object      g;
}

dimensions      [0 1 -2 0 0 0 0];

g   (-9.81 0 0);
```

The key change is changing `class uniformDimensionedVectorField;` instead of `class vectorField;`. The `uniformDimensionedVectorField` type tells OpenFOAM that this is a uniform (spatially constant) vector field with dimensional units, which is what `buoyantFoam` expects for the gravity vector.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `constant/g` | Change `class vectorField;` → `class uniformDimensionedVectorField;` |

This is the standard format for specifying a uniform gravity vector in OpenFOAM. The `buoyantFoam` solver reads this field at startup to set up the buoyancy forces. All previous attempts focused on missing fields in `0/` or patch naming issues, but this error is about the file format/type in `constant/g`.</reviewer_analysis>

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1005;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 305;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }
}

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;

The key change is changing `class uniformDimensionedVectorField;` instead of `class vectorField;`. The `uniformDimensionedVectorField` type tells OpenFOAM that this is a uniform (spatially constant) vector field with dimensional units, which is what `buoyantFoam` expects for the gravity vector.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `constant/g` | Change `class vectorField;` → `class uniformDimensionedVectorField;` |

This is the standard format for specifying a uniform gravity vector in OpenFOAM. The `buoyantFoam` solver reads this field at startup to set up the buoyancy forces. All previous attempts focused on missing fields in `0/` or patch naming issues, but this error is about the file format/type in `constant/g`.
</Review_Analysis>

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1005;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 305;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

{

    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }
}

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword value is undefined in dictionary "constant/g"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is looking for a keyword (or entry) named `g` inside the `constant/g` dictionary, but it cannot find it. The solver is trying to read `g` as a dictionary entry rather than as a standalone file.

## Root Cause

The `buoyantFoam` solver (which is based on `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam`) expects the gravity vector `g` to be defined as a **dictionary entry** within the `constant/` directory's main dictionary file (likely `physicalProperties`), not as a separate file. The solver reads the `constant/` directory as a dictionary and looks for entries within it, rather than reading separate files.

Looking at the current `constant/physicalProperties` file, there is no `g` entry. The solver is trying to find `g` as a keyword within the `physicalProperties` dictionary, but it doesn't exist there.

## Proposed Solution

**Add the gravity vector `g` as an entry in the `constant/physicalProperties` dictionary**, rather than as a separate file. Update the `physicalProperties` file to include:

```
g   (-9.81 0 0);
```

The updated `physicalProperties` file should look like:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1005;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}

g   (-9.81 0 0);
```

The key change is adding the line `g   (-9.81 0 0);` at the top level of the `physicalProperties` dictionary (outside of any sub-dictionary like `thermoType` or `mixture`). This is how OpenFOAM's `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam` (and thus `buoyantFoam`) expects to find the gravity vector — as a dictionary entry in `physicalProperties`.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `constant/physicalProperties` | **Add** `g   (-9.81 0 0);` as a top-level entry in the main dictionary |

This is the standard way OpenFOAM's buoyancy solvers expect gravity to be specified — as a dictionary entry in `physicalProperties`, not as a separate file. All previous attempts focused on missing fields in `0/` or patch naming issues, or the format of the `g` file. This approach changes the location of the gravity definition from a separate file to the correct dictionary entry within `physicalProperties`.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword value is undefined in dictionary "constant/g"
```

This means the `buoyantFoam` solver is looking for a keyword (or entry) named `g` inside the `constant/g` dictionary, but it cannot find it. The solver is trying to read `g` as a dictionary entry rather than as a standalone file.

## Root Cause

The `buoyantFoam` solver (which is based on `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam`) expects the gravity vector `g` to be defined as a **dictionary entry** within the `constant/` directory's main dictionary file (likely `physicalProperties`), not as a separate file. The solver reads the `constant/` directory as a dictionary and looks for entries within it, rather than reading separate files.

Looking at the current `constant/physicalProperties` file, there is no `g` entry. The solver is trying to find `g` as a keyword within the `physicalProperties` dictionary, but it doesn't exist there.

## Proposed Solution

**Add the gravity vector `g` as an entry in the `constant/physicalProperties` dictionary**, rather than as a separate file. Update the `physicalProperties` file to include:

```
g   (-9.81 0 0);
```

The updated `physicalProperties` file should look like:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   28.9;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          1005;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          1.831e-05;
        Pr          0.705;
    }
}

g   (-9.81 0 0);
```

The key change is adding the line `g   (-9.81 0 0);` at the top level of the `physicalProperties` dictionary (outside of any sub-dictionary like `thermoType` or `mixture`). This is how OpenFOAM's `buoyantBoussinesqSimpleFoam` (and thus `buoyantFoam`) expects to find the gravity vector — as a dictionary entry in `physicalProperties`.

## Summary of Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `constant/physicalProperties` | **Add** `g   (-9.81 0 0);` as a top-level entry in the main dictionary |

This is the standard way OpenFOAM's buoyancy solvers expect gravity to be specified — as a dictionary entry in `physicalProperties`, not as a separate file. All previous attempts focused on missing fields in `0/` or patch naming issues, or the format of the `g` file. This approach changes the location of the gravity definition from a separate file to the correct dictionary entry within `physicalProperties`.</reviewer_analysis>

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|omega)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           kEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}

internalField   uniform 300;

boundaryField
{
    topWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 305;
    }

    bottomWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    backWall
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 300;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}
